assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Regular channel clusters will auto-upgrade to the next available version, with timeline of 1-2 weeks after EoS

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response mentions Extended channel clusters continue running 1.30 for additional months and have more time before forced upgrades

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states No Channel clusters will remain on 1.30 indefinitely and require manual action, missing the force-upgrade behavior for both control plane and nodes at EoS

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: No mention of maintenance exclusions or ability to temporarily defer EoS force-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response provides clear action plan recommending manual upgrades before auto-upgrades and immediate action for No Channel cluster

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: No mention of Cloud Logging notifications for EoS planning

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: No mention of deprecation insights dashboard or API compatibility checking

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: While it mentions security risks for No Channel clusters, it doesn't explicitly warn against snowflaking or manually freezing versions as a general anti-pattern
